Usage: gaia [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

 Gaia — knowledge package authoring toolkit.

╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --version                     Show version, channel, commit, and ir_schema;  │
│                               then exit.                                     │
│ --install-completion          Install completion for the current shell.      │
│ --show-completion             Show completion for the current shell, to copy │
│                               it or customize the installation.              │
│ --help                        Show this message and exit.                    │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ build    Build artifacts (init / compile / check).                           │
│ run      Run inference and rendering (infer / render).                       │
│ inspect  Inspect compiled artifacts (starmap / starmap-replay).              │
│ review   Reviewer tooling (alpha 0: skeleton only — no commands yet).        │
│ inquiry  Gaia Inquiry — semantic review loop (focus / review / reject /      │
│          obligation / hypothesis / tactics).                                 │
│ pkg      Package operations (add / add-import / add-module / register /      │
│          scaffold).                                                          │
│ author   Author DSL statements (claim / equal / derive / note / question /   │
│          contradict / exclusive / decompose / observe / compute / infer /    │
│          associate / parameter / register-prior / variable / depends-on /    │
│          candidate-relation / materialize / compose / composition / list).   │
│ bayes    Bayesian-modelling authoring (model / compare / Binomial /          │
│          BetaBinomial / Normal / LogNormal / Beta / Exponential / Gamma /    │
│          StudentT / Cauchy / ChiSquared / Poisson).                          │
│ example  Print or save the cli walkthrough script for a shipping example     │
│          package (galileo / mendel).                                         │
│ skill    Materialise bundled Gaia skills into cwd (register / list).         │
│ trace    Gaia ARM Trace — verify and review execution traces (verify /       │
│          review / show).                                                     │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

 What gaia does:
 Gaia turns a structured argument — claims, observations, derivations — into a
 compiled Bayesian network. Author packages incrementally with `gaia author …`,
 compile with `gaia build compile`, run inference with `gaia run infer`, and
 render a slide-deck-ready artifact with `gaia run render`. See `gaia example
 galileo` for an end-to-end walkthrough.
 What is a knowledge package?
 A knowledge package is a small Python project that captures a structured
 argument — premises, derivations, observations — that gaia compiles into a
 Bayesian network. The shipped examples (`gaia example galileo`, `gaia example
 mendel`) walk through building one end-to-end.
 Typical authoring flow:
 $ gaia build init my-pkg-gaia
 $ gaia author claim "..." --target ./my-pkg-gaia
 $ gaia build compile ./my-pkg-gaia
 $ gaia run infer ./my-pkg-gaia
 Run `gaia <group> --help` for per-group verb references.
 Run `gaia example --help` to see how a full demo is authored.
